assembly_info.comments:
The assembly icAmaAene1.hap1.1 is based on 126x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project
(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosome X was identified by copy number in the diploid assembly. There was no identifiable Y sequence in the assembly however it may be present in the unplaced scaffolds or the species may be XO.

assembly_info.comments: Genome assembly of Androsace saussurei. This assembly is provided by the European Reference Genome Atlas (ERGA, https://www.erga-biodiversity.eu/) via the Biodiversity Genomics Europe project (BGE, https://biodiversitygenomics.eu/). Several ploidies were observed in the Androsace genus, with 10 chromosomes (Weiss (2021), "Chromosome number and ploidy level of Androsace maxima (Primulaceae) in Austria", https://www.zobodat.at/pdf/NEIL_1_0177-0180.pdf). Here, we reconstructed 40 chromosomes (clearly 4 sets of 10 chromosomes based on the Hi-C map), suggesting a tetraploid or an octoploid genome for Androsace saussurei. If the genome is tetraploid, it is unusual as the assembler reconstructed complete maternal and paternal alleles, indicating high heterozygosity. In contrast, if the genome is octoploid, it indicates near homozygosity. We can clearly see regions with higher coverage that may indicate gene conversions between the homologous chromosomes, which would require additional techniques for complete assembly. Tetraploidy was also suggested by Smudgeplot, and SNPs showed few variations, supporting the tetraploid hypothesis. The twenty most complete chromosomes will be submitted as the reference haplotype, and the other twenty as the alternate haplotype.

assembly_info.comments: Genome assembly of Androsace saussurei (alternate haplotype). This assembly is provided by the European Reference Genome Atlas (ERGA, https://www.erga-biodiversity.eu/) via the Biodiversity Genomics Europe project (BGE, https://biodiversitygenomics.eu/). Several ploidies were observed in the Androsace genus, with 10 chromosomes (Weiss (2021), "Chromosome number and ploidy level of Androsace maxima (Primulaceae) in Austria", https://www.zobodat.at/pdf/NEIL_1_0177-0180.pdf). Here, we reconstructed 40 chromosomes (clearly 4 sets of 10 chromosomes based on the Hi-C map), suggesting a tetraploid or an octoploid genome for Androsace saussurei. If the genome is tetraploid, it is unusual as the assembler reconstructed complete maternal and paternal alleles, indicating high heterozygosity. In contrast, if the genome is octoploid, it indicates near homozygosity. We can clearly see regions with higher coverage that may indicate gene conversions between the homologous chromosomes, which would require additional techniques for complete assembly. Tetraploidy was also suggested by Smudgeplot, and SNPs showed few variations, supporting the tetraploid hypothesis. The twenty most complete chromosomes will be submitted as the reference haplotype, and the other twenty as the alternate haplotype.

assembly_info.comments:
The assembly ieBaeAtla2.hap1.1 is based on 119x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project
(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Multiple haplotypic differences were observed between the different samples. As example large inversions on chromosome 1 (31.7-35 Mbp) and chromosome 2 (20.3-33.5 Mbp), as well as translocations like chromosome 4 540-646kbp to chromosome 3 40.2. These only exist in the HiC sample and not the PacBio one and do not exist between the two haplotypes of the PacBio assembly.

assembly_info.comments:
The assembly dmBerVulg1.1 is based on 28x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project
(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. the exact order and orientation of the centromeric repeat regions is unknown.

assembly_info.comments:
The assembly dmBerVulg1.1 is based on 28x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project
(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. This alternate haplotype assembly combines the haplotigs separated by purge_dups with haplotigs separated from the primary assembly during curation. the exact order and orientation of the centromeric repeat regions is unknown.

assembly_info.comments: The assembly ilCloChar1.hap2.1 is based on 31x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosomes Z and W were identified by copy number in the diploid assembly. The order and orientation of scaffolds making up the W chromosomes is uncertain. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 21 (6.0 - 6.6 Mbp), chromosome 21 (7.2 - 7.8 Mbp) are unknown.

assembly_info.comments: The assembly ilBolEuno1.hap1.1 is based on 24x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosomes Z and W were identified by copy number in the diploid assembly and by HiC signal. The assembly records a Z+autosome and W+autosome fusion.

assembly_info.comments:
The assembly ilBolEuph2.2 is based on 65x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project
(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. This alternate haplotype assembly combines the haplotigs separated by purge_dups with haplotigs separated from the primary assembly during curation.

The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHifi. Finally, the assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

description.comment: Single male specimen of the Giant Patagonian bumblebee, collected at the end of the season (when most fertilized queens have already gone into their overwintering refuges), from a large plant of chilco (Fuchsia magellanica) on its late summer/early fall flowering pulse. At this point of the year, most queens born during the summer season have been fertilized and gone into overwintering refuges; most individuals of the species are males living outside nests, and grouping on the few remaining flowering plants available. The male was preserved in molecular grade 100% ethanol, and sent to Dovetail Genomics for DNA extraction and sequencing.

assembly_info.comments: The assembly iyBomHort1.1 is based on 82x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Arima Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHifi. Finally, the assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

assembly_info.comments: 'Bombus ruderatus was assembled with Hifiasm (version 0.25.0-r726). Contaminations were removed with FCS-GX (version 0.5.5) based on the ALL database released on 2023/01. The manual curation was done with PretextView (version 1.0.3) and GRIT_Rapid (git: 1a3d79a8).'

assembly_info.comments:
Contact ncbi-contacts@bcm.tmc.edu for more information.
The DNA source was a single haploid drone for the XLR fragment library and pools of haploid drones for the 20kb and 8kb libraries. Drones were provided by Paul Schmid-Hempel.
Bter_1.0 was created with 454 Newbler assembler v. 2.3-PreRelease-10/19/2009 for the shotgun sequences, followed by local re-assembly with additional unassembled mate-pairs using Phrap to close additional gaps.

bioprojects.title: Bombus terrestris (buff-tailed bumblebee) genome assembly, iyBomTerr1
Darwin Tree of Life Project: Genome Data and Assemblies
Sanger Institute Tree of Life Programme: Data and Assemblies

assembly_info.comments: The assembly iyBomTerr1.2 is based on 57x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Arima Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHifi. Finally, the assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds are named by synteny based on Bombus terrestris (buff-tailed bumblebee) GCA_000214255.1. Many unplaced scaffolds are composed entirely of a 10mer repeat which we note occurs in other Bombus assemblies.

bioprojects.title: Bombus vestalis (vestal cuckoo bee) genome assembly, iyBomVest1
Darwin Tree of Life Project: Genome Data and Assemblies
Sanger Institute Tree of Life Programme: Data and Assemblies

assembly_info.comments:
The assembly iyBomVest1.1 is based on 111x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project
(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi and OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

assembly_info.comments: Genome assembly of Carabus banonii. This assembly is provided by the European Reference Genome Atlas (ERGA, https://www.erga-biodiversity.eu/) via the Biodiversity Genomics Europe project (BGE, https://biodiversitygenomics.eu/). No significant bacterial contamination in the assembly. But some small scaffolds for which no HiC links was found were removed. Presence of larges repeated regions (telomeric or centromeric?) at the ends of the scaffolds,therefore need to invert the arms of chromosomes.

(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosome X was assigned based on synteny to GCA_947425015.1

assembly_info.comments:
The assembly lpCarCary1.hap1.2 is based on 39x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project
(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 1 (0 - 1.96 Mbp) are unknown.

assembly_info.comments:
The assembly lpCarNigr3.hap1.1 is based on 51x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project
(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The region of Chromosome 22 from start to 2.3 Mbp appears to be collapsed by the assembler and should be represented in two copy but is only present in single copy in this assembly. The following regions appear to be haplotypic inversions: Chromosome 22
0.2-0.9 Mbp, Chromosome 10
2.7-6.2 Mbp. Chromosome B1 is a putative B chromosome.

assembly_info.comments:
The assembly icCatNigr1.hap1.1 is based on 102x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project
(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome has been assembled using PacBio and HiC data and phased. The result is two curated haplotypes. The X chromosome was identified by read coverage, no Y chromosome was found. A large number of centromeric repeats is in the unlocalised sequences. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 1 (1.7 - 5.8 Mbp) and chromosome 4 (8.8 - 9.6 Mbp) are unknown. A haplotypic inversion was observed in the region on chromosome 8 (8.1-8.5 Mbp).

assembly_info.comments: The assembly glClaPort1.1 is based on 66x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Aquatic Symbiosis Genomics Project (https://www.aquaticsymbiosisgenomics.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi and OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

bioprojects.title: Large-scale analysis of novel cellular microbes from the lichen biome
Super study for large-scale analysis of novel cellular microbes

attributes.name: assembly quality
attributes.value: Many fragments with little to no review of assembly other than reporting of standard assembly statistics

attributes.name: project_name
attributes.value: Reductions in genome size and complexity are a hallmark of obligate symbioses. The mitochondrial genome displays clear examples of these reductions, with the ancestral alpha-proteobacterial genome size and gene number having been reduced by orders of magnitude in most descendent modern mitochondrial genomes. Here, we examine patterns of mitochondrial evolution specifically looking at intron size, number, and position across 58 species from 21 genera of lichenized Ascomycete fungi, representing a broad range of fungal diversity and niches. Our results show that the cox1 gene always contained the highest number of introns out of all the mitochondrial protein-coding genes, that high intron sequence similarity can be maintained between different genera, and that lichens have undergone at least two instances of complete, genome-wide intron loss consistent with evidence for genome streamlining via loss of parasitic, noncoding DNA, in Phlyctis boliviensis and Graphis lineola. Notably, however, lichenized fungi have not only undergone intron loss but in some instances have expanded considerably in size due to intron proliferation (e.g., Alectoria fallacina and Parmotrema neotropicum), even between closely related sister species (e.g., Cladonia). These results shed light on the highly dynamic mitochondrial evolution that is occurring in lichens and suggest that these obligate symbiotic organisms are in some cases undergoing recent, broad-scale genome streamlining via loss of protein-coding genes as well as noncoding, parasitic DNA elements.

These results shed light on the highly dynamic mitochondrial evolution that is occurring in lichens and suggest that these obligate symbiotic organisms are in some cases undergoing recent, broad-scale genome streamlining via loss of protein-coding genes as well as noncoding, parasitic DNA elements.

assembly_info.comments:
The assembly glClaNorv1.1 is based on 369x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project
(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

bioprojects.title: Genome sequencing of Cladophialophora bantiana UM 956 by Illumina sequencing platform

description.comment: Cladophialophora bantiana is a dematiaceous fungus that cause central nervous system (CNS) infection in both immunocompetent and immunocompromised patients. In this work, C. bantiana UM 956 was recovered from the cerebrospinal fluid of a patient with brain abscess. The draft genome of the C. bantiana was generated by a combined assembly of two different insert-size Illumina sequencing libraries (5-kb insert-size library and 500-bp insert-size library). The generated genome will serve a platform to further understand its basic biology and its pathogenicity in human.

Super study for large-scale analysis of novel cellular microbes

attributes.name: assembly quality
attributes.value: Many fragments with little to no review of assembly other than reporting of standard assembly statistics

attributes.name: project_name
attributes.value: Wine is a complex beverage, comprised of thousands of metabolites that are produced through the action of yeasts and bacteria in fermenting grape must. To ensure a robust and reliable fermentation, most commercial wines are produced via inoculation with large amounts of the major wine yeast, Saccharomyces cerevisiae. However, there is a growing trend towards the use of uninoculated or “wild” fermentations, in which the yeasts and bacteria that are naturally associated with the vineyard or winery perform the fermentation. The varied metabolic contributions of the numerous non-Saccharomyces species in this microbial community are thought to impart complexity and desirable taste and aroma attributes to wild ferments when compared to their inoculated counterparts.</p><p>In order the map the microflora of spontaneous fermentation, metagenomic techniques were used to characterise and monitor the progression of fungal species in several wild fermentations. Both amplicon-based ITS phylotyping and shotgun metagenomics were used to assess community structure, in addition to the isolation, sequencing and de novo assembly of the first reference genomes for several dominant wine-associated species.

However, there is a growing trend towards the use of uninoculated or “wild” fermentations, in which the yeasts and bacteria that are naturally associated with the vineyard or winery perform the fermentation. The varied metabolic contributions of the numerous non-Saccharomyces species in this microbial community are thought to impart complexity and desirable taste and aroma attributes to wild ferments when compared to their inoculated counterparts.</p><p>In order the map the microflora of spontaneous fermentation, metagenomic techniques were used to characterise and monitor the progression of fungal species in several wild fermentations. Both amplicon-based ITS phylotyping and shotgun metagenomics were used to assess community structure, in addition to the isolation, sequencing and de novo assembly of the first reference genomes for several dominant wine-associated species.

attributes.name: project_name
attributes.value:
16S rRNA gene sequences amplified from subjects with eczema and age-matched healthy controls.

Microbes living in and on humans are ten times more numerous than human cells. Culture-based methods have been the primary techniques used to study microbes inhabiting humans however, many species are not successfully grown in culture. The NIH Roadmap for Medical Research Human Microbiome Project (HMP) aims to investigate the microbes in the gastrointestinal tract, oral cavity, skin, vagina, and nares. The goal of the HMP is to comprehensively characterize the human microbiota and analyze its role in human health and disease.

The skin serves not only as a barrier against invading pathogens and moisture loss, but also as a host to microbial communities. The skin of an adult is an approximately 2 square meter surface with a myriad of microenvironments. Atopic dermatitis (AD), more commonly known as eczema, is a common skin disorder that is exacerbated by Staphylococcus aureus colonization. This study aims to investigate the skin microbiota of AD patients at specific timepoints (quiescence, disease flares, and post-treatment) to examine how disease state correlates with changes in the skin microflora.

The 16S small subunit ribosomal (rRNA) gene is present in every bacterial cell, serving as a universal marker. The gene is sufficiently conserved to allow accurate alignment but adequately varied to enable phylogenetic analyses. Sequencing via 16S rRNA-based phylotyping has been used to survey the bacterial microbes on the skin. Initially, we will examine the bacterial diversity associated with AD using a 16S rRNA survey and later broadening to include fungi, viruses, archaea and mites. In addition, bacteria will be cultured on a wide variety of media and isolates representing both abundant and novel species will be selected for whole genome sequencing.

To characterize fungal diversity, we utilize two phylogenetic markers within the rDNA region: 18S rRNA and the Intervening Internal Transcribed Spacer (ITS) region. Full microbial diversity will be explored with shotgun metagenomic sequencing.

annotation_info.name: Annotation submitted by ELIXIR Norway

assembly_info.comments: This project provides the diploid genome assembly idDiaHype1.1 of Diamesa hyperborea. The sample was provided by Elisabeth Stur & Torbjorn Ekrem, and the data generation and assembly were carried out by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). idDiaHype1.1 is based on 62x PacBio HiFi data and 99x Arima Hi-C data. The HiFi reads were filtered for adapters using HiFiAdapterFilt, and assembled with hifiasm along with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to create two sets of reads for scaffolding the assemblies (i.e., reads used to scaffold hap1 did not contain k-mers unique to hap2). YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters, and putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes confirmed by Hi-C data are named according to size.

assembly_info.comments:
The assembly drEpiCili1.hap1.1 is based on 35x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project
(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. bioprojects.title: European Reference Genome Atlas (ERGA): Biodiversity Genomics Europe (BGE) Project Genome Data and Assemblies

assembly_info.comments: The assembly of Formica gagates (iyForGaga1) is based on 72X PacBio data and Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the European Reference Genome Atlas (ERGA, https://www.erga-biodiversity.eu/) via the Biodiversity Genomics Europe project (BGE, https://biodiversitygenomics.eu/).The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 5 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 2.8 Mb (with the largest being 1.4 Mb). The mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK but the sequence obtained is linear. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 5 haplotypic regions were removed,totaling 0.903 Mb (with the largest being 0.539 Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size

assembly_info.comments:
The assembly gfInoGrao2.1 is based on 432x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project
(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

biosample.identified_by: PENNY CULLINGTON | KIERAN WOOF | BRIAN DOUGLAS

assembly_info.comments:
The assembly gfInoObsi1.1 is based on 225x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project
(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MBG from PacBio HiFi reads mapping to related genomes. A representative sequence was selected for each from the graph based on read coverage, contig size, and its alignments to the related genomes, then MitoHiFi was run on this sequence for circularisation and annotation with MitoFinder. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

assembly_info.comments:
The assembly gfLacTabi1.1 is based on 459x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project
(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MBG from PacBio HiFi reads mapping to related genomes. A representative sequence was selected for each from the graph based on read coverage, contig size, and its alignments to the related genomes, then MitoHiFi was run on this sequence for circularisation and annotation with MitoFinder. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

assembly_info.comments:
The assembly daLobUren4.hap2.2 is based on 168x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project
(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome has been assembled using PacBio and HiC data and phased. The result is two curated haplotypes. A haplotypic inversion and translocation was observed in the region on chromosome 3 ( 51.7-53.9 Mbp). A large number of centromeric repeats is in the unlocalised sequences. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 5 (30.0 - 31.1 Mbp ) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 7 (25.2 - 26.1 Mbp) are unknown.

assembly_info.comments: The assembly icNebBrev1.1 is based on 70x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The order and orientation of scaffolds within the centromeric region of chromosomes 1, 3, 4, 8 and 10 is uncertain.

bioprojects.title: Nitzschia sp. DOCU1 genome assembly, pyDurOcul1.Nitzschia_sp_1, alternate haplotype
Aquatic Symbiosis Genomics Project: Data and Assemblies
Sanger Institute Tree of Life Programme: Data and Assemblies

assembly_info.comments: The assembly pyDurOcul1.Nitzschia_sp_1.1 is based on 250x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Aquatic Symbiosis Genomics Project (https://www.aquaticsymbiosisgenomics.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. This alternate haplotype assembly combines the haplotigs separated by purge_dups with haplotigs separated from the primary assembly during curation.

Darwin Tree of Life Project: Genome Data and Assemblies
Sanger Institute Tree of Life Programme: Data and Assemblies
AEGIS (Ancient Environmental Genomics Initiative for Sustainability)

assembly_info.comments:
The assembly dcOxyDigy1.hap1.1 is based on 216x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project
(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. the exact order and orientation of the contigs in the repetetive sequence of chromosome 5 (73.5 Mbp - end) is unknown. Multiple heterozygous inversions were observed.

assembly_info.comments:
The assembly icPhrVite1.1 is based on 44x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project
(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. A manually phased assembly (based on HiC signal) was generated from a jointly curated primary and alt assembly. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. X chromosome identified based on BUSCO gene painting with ancestral coleopteran linkage groups.

attributes.value: leaves were cut and put into FluidX tubes

assembly_info.comments:
The assembly lpPoaChai1.hap1.1 is based on 40x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project
(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. B1 is a putative supernumerary B chromosome and has higher pacbio read coverage than the autosomes. Chromosome 1 has a heterozygous inversion between 200.7 Mb and 212.5 Mb.

assembly_info.comments:
The assembly gfRusFari1.1 is based on 513x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project
(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MBG from PacBio HiFi reads mapping to related genomes. A representative sequence was selected for each from the graph based on read coverage, contig size, and its alignments to the related genomes, then MitoHiFi was run on this sequence for circularisation and annotation with MitoFinder. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

assembly_info.comments:
The assembly gfRusDeli1.hap1.1 is based on 434x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project
(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. There is an inversion between haplotypes on Chromosome 9 between approximately 1.6-1.9Mb

The assembly ddSalHerb6.1 is based on 53x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project
(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. A manually phased assembly (based on HiC signal) was generated from a jointly curated primary and alt assembly. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

assembly_info.comments: The assembly drSaxVayr1.hap1.1 is based on 140x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated as part of the European Reference Genome Atlas (ERGA, https://www.erga-biodiversity.eu/) via the Biodiversity Genomics Europe project (BGE, https://biodiversitygenomics.eu/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The sample appears to be allopolyploid (4n = 58) with the HIC signal indicating highly divergent subgenomes. The primary (HAP 1) assembly contains a single copy of each subgenome. The order and orientation of contigs along chromosome 1 is uncertain between 15 Mb and 21.5 Mb. Heterozygous inversions were observed in the following regions: chromosome 7, 5.3 Mb to 7 Mb.; chromosome 8, 8.2 Mb to 10.9 Mb and 15.6 Mb to 17. 6 Mb.

attributes.name: submission description
attributes.value: The project's primary goal is to sequence 25 novel genomes representing UK biodiversity, as part of the Wellcome Sanger Institute's wider 25th Anniversary celebrations. This project will be a pathfinder for future long-read sequencing projects to demonstrate the Institute’s capabilities and will provide reference genomes for the global scientific community.

assembly_info.comments: The assembly dSenSqu2.1 is based on 43x PacBio data and 10X Genomics Chromium data generated at the Wellcome Sanger Institute, and Hi-C data generated by Dovetail Genomics. The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Falcon-unzip, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, 10X based scaffolding with scaff10x, Hi-C based scaffolding using HiRise, Arrow polishing, and two rounds of FreeBayes polishing. The chloroplast assembly was generated using Novoplasty by Bruno Nevado at the University of Oxford.

assembly_info.comments:
The assembly daSenSylv1.hap1.1 is based on 26x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project
(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome has been assembled using PacBio and HiC data and phased. The result is two curated haplotypes. A haplotypic inversion was observed in the region on chromosome 18 (47.8-55.5 Mbp). A large number of centromeric repeats is in the unlocalised sequences. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 2 (43 - 48 Mbp), chromosome 5 (40 - 44 Mbp), chromosome 13 (53.9-58.1 Mbp), chromosome 14 (34.8 - 38.8 Mbp) are unknown.

description.comment: In some species, the Y is a tiny chromosome but the dioecious plant Silene latifolia has a giant ~550 Mb Y chromosome, which has remained unsequenced so far. Here we used a hybrid approach to obtain a high-quality male S. latifolia genome. Using mutants for sexual phenotype, we identified candidate sex-determining genes on the Y. Comparative analysis of the sex chromosomes with outgroups showed the Y is surprisingly rearranged and degenerated for a ~11 MY-old system. Recombination suppression between X and Y extended in a stepwise process, and triggered a massive accumulation of repeats on the Y, as well as in the non-recombining pericentromeric region of the X, leading to giant sex chromosomes.
description.title: Plant sample from Silene latifolia

bioprojects.title: Darwin Tree of Life Project: Genome Data and Assemblies
Sanger Institute Tree of Life Programme: Data and Assemblies

assembly_info.comments:
The assembly idTipConf1.1 is based on 32x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project
(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. This alternate haplotype assembly combines the haplotigs separated by purge_dups with haplotigs separated from the primary assembly during curation.

assembly_info.comments:
The assembly idTipHelv1.1 is based on 22x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project
(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Sample sexed as male but no half coverage (sex) chromosomes observed. Possible misidentification or presence of homomorphic sex chromosomes.

attributes.value: NATURAL HISTORY MUSEUM | NATURAL HISTORY MUSEUM

assembly_info.comments:
The assembly idTipLate1.1 is based on 48x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project
(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. All chromosomes are named in size order. We did not identify the sex chromosome(s) as sequence data from the heterogametic sex was not available and homology is unreliable for sex chromosome identification in Diptera due to frequent sex chromosome turnover (https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pbio.1002078).

bioprojects.title: Darwin Tree of Life Project: Genome Data and Assemblies
Sanger Institute Tree of Life Programme: Data and Assemblies

assembly_info.comments:
The assembly idTipScri1.hap2.1 is based on 114x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project
(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome has been assembled using PacBio and HiC data and phased. The result is two curated haplotypes. A haplotypic inversion was observed in the regions on chromosome 2 ( 61-90, 110-124, 150-169 Mbp).

assembly_info.comments: The assembly ilZygFili1.2 is based on 58x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Arima Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHifi. Finally, the assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

bioprojects.title: European Reference Genome Atlas (ERGA): Biodiversity Genomics Europe (BGE) Project Genome Data and Assemblies

assembly_info.comments: The assembly of Zygaena laeta (ilZygLaet5.1) is based on 44X PacBio data and 354X of Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the European Reference Genome Atlas (ERGA, https://www.erga-biodiversity.eu/) via the Biodiversity Genomics Europe project (BGE, https://biodiversitygenomics.eu/). The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 1 contig of 0.016 Mb was identified as contaminant (bacterial). Additionally, 503 regions totaling 37.471 Mb (with the largest being 0.451 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 7 haplotypic regions and 1 contaminant sequence were removed, totaling 3.478 Mb and 0.027 Mb (with the largest being 0.611 Mb and 0.027 Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.
